key_insight: The paper shows that LLM-driven agents can autonomously manage the full HPC application workflow in cloud, from container build to Kubernetes deployment and scaling studies.
engineering_relevance: This points to a practical path for accelerating materials discovery workflows by reducing manual effort in HPC setup, optimization, and experimental execution.
method: Built agents to create and build containers for HPC applications
Deployed and orchestrated runs in Kubernetes across cloud instances
Optimized and executed scaling studies over 21 instance types
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful linear scaling with human-approved patterns and improved time-to-completion across four well-known HPC applications.
watch_item: Performance and reliability still depend on careful human oversight, since agentic decisions may not generalize cleanly across applications, platforms, or cluster configurations.
paper_title: Agentic Orchestration of HPC Applications in Cloud
